macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stock Markets Fall Amid Iran Tensions and Oil Surge
items.summary: Major U.S. and Canadian stock indices declined on March 3, 2026, as Iran launched retaliatory attacks including a drone strike on the U.S. Embassy in Saudi Arabia. The S&P 500 fell 105.66 points to 6,775.96, while Canada's S&P/TSX composite dropped over 1,000 points to 33,359.05.

items.title: Crude Oil Prices Spike on Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices surged significantly as Iran's retaliatory strikes escalated Middle East tensions. The April crude oil contract rose US$6.25 to US$77.48 per barrel, while gold declined US$176.30 to US$5,135.30 per ounce.

items.title: S&P 500 Struggles Below 6,900 Pivot Point
items.summary: The S&P 500 faces technical resistance at the 6,900 level, with weakness in this zone potentially signaling a decline toward 6,730 during a seasonally weak period for equities. The 100-day moving average at 6,833 provides key support.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Top 100 Crypto Tokens Show Mixed Performance with RIVER Surging 22%
items.summary: RIVER led gains with a 22.48% surge to $17.67, followed by NEAR (+4.7%), XDC (+4.07%), MNT (+2.97%), and CHZ (+1.96%). Losers included PIPPIN (-18.88%), AAVE (-10.52%), and others down over 9%.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady at $66K Amid Crude Spike and Equities Slide
items.summary: BTC traded at $66,615, up 0.31% as of 09:30 UTC, with most major cryptocurrencies showing mixed performance. Market outperformers were highlighted amid broader financial market movements.[5]

items.title: 38% of Altcoins Trade Near Record Lows
items.summary: Nearly 38% of altcoins are trading close to their all-time low levels, signaling ongoing pressure in the altcoin market despite some gains in top performers.[4]

items.title: TCS Blockchain and PayPal Partner for On-Chain Freight Settlements Using PYUSD
items.summary: TCS Blockchain, processing over $1B in freight invoices annually, partners with PayPal to use TCS Token on INX-Republic and PYUSD stablecoin for faster, cheaper settlements in the $3T trucking industry, offering up to 90% savings.[3]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Surpasses Creator's World Record in Tempest Game
items.summary: A custom AI developed over a year achieved a groundbreaking milestone by surpassing its creator's world record in Tempest, utilizing 15 million frames of gameplay and innovative techniques like attention mechanisms and polar coordinates.

items.title: Alibaba Qwen Unveils Qwen 3.5 Medium Models
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen announced new 3.5 Medium models that claim to deliver more intelligence while requiring less computational resources.

items.title: AMD and Meta Expand GPU Partnership to 6GW
items.summary: Meta is partnering with AMD to rapidly scale AI infrastructure and accelerate development and deployment of cutting-edge AI models through a major GPU deployment initiative.

items.title: Huawei Launches Next-Generation Voice Virtual Agents for AI Contact Centers
items.summary: Huawei unveiled advanced voice virtual agents with hyper-human interaction capabilities at MWC Barcelona 2026, promising to improve self-service resolution rates by 20% across industries.

items.title: University of North Carolina Launches AI-Powered Antibody Therapy Research
items.summary: Carolina researchers received funding to use AI and machine learning models to replace traditional animal studies in antibody drug development, reducing costs and time to market.

items.title: VIAVI Showcases AI Fabric and Optical Test Advances at OFC 2026
items.summary: VIAVI Solutions will demonstrate breakthrough technologies for validating next-generation AI fabrics, including 1.6T high-speed Ethernet infrastructure and distributed acoustic sensing with edge-based AI capabilities.

items.title: ZTE Showcases Full-Stack AI Innovations at MWC Barcelona 2026
items.summary: ZTE highlighted deep integration of AI and ICT across four core areas: AI Agentic Connectivity, AI Cloud, AI Home, and Smart Personal Devices.

items.title: CEOs Identify AI as Top Business Risk
items.summary: For the first time, corporate executives rank artificial intelligence as the biggest business risk, surpassing concerns about geopolitical turmoil and cyber threats.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Indie World Showcase March 2026 Held
items.summary: Nintendo held its March 2026 Indie World Showcase on March 3, featuring approximately 15 minutes of announcements for upcoming indie games on Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. Notable announcements included Rotwood's simultaneous release on Nintendo Switch 2 and Drinkbox Studios' action-RPG Blighted coming to Switch 2 this fall.

items.title: Multiple Games Launch on Xbox Game Pass in Early March
items.summary: Xbox Game Pass added several titles including Final Fantasy III, Kingdom Come: Deliverance II, Planet of Lana II (March 5), and Cyberpunk 2077 (March 10). Call of Duty: Warzone also received a new Black Ops Royale mode launching March 13.

items.title: Major Nintendo Switch 2 Games Releasing This Month
items.summary: Nintendo announced several major March 2026 releases including Scott Pilgrim EX (March 4), Pokémon Pokopia (March 5), Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (March 13), WWE 2K26 (March 13), and Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O. World Stage (March 26).

items.title: Rotwood Released on Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Rotwood was announced and simultaneously released on Nintendo Switch 2 during the March 3, 2026 Nintendo Indie World Showcase, following its Early Access launch on Steam in April 2024.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Introduces New MacBook Air with M5 Chip
items.summary: Apple announced the MacBook Air with M5 processor, featuring faster CPU and GPU performance, double the starting storage, and improved wireless connectivity. The new models are available in 13- and 15-inch sizes with pre-orders starting March 4 and availability beginning March 11.

items.title: B2B Technology Market Forecast to Grow 3% in 2026
items.summary: Circana projects the B2B technology market will grow 3% in 2026, driven by premium and AI-PC configurations, continued cloud expansion, and enterprise modernization efforts. Hardware, software, and cloud services are all expected to see growth.

items.title: Huawei Showcases 115 Industrial Intelligence Solutions at MWC 2026
items.summary: Huawei released 115 industrial intelligence showcases and launched 22 new industrial intelligence solutions at MWC Barcelona 2026, spanning sectors including electric power, manufacturing, retail, finance, and transportation. The company also upgraded its SHAPE 2.0 partner framework with enhanced AI capabilities.

items.title: Alberta Budget Allocates $5.5 Million for Government AI Adoption
items.summary: Alberta's 2026 budget includes $5.5 million for AI adoption across government services and $40 million for critical cybersecurity upgrades. The budget also introduces a data centre levy of up to 2% expected to generate $102 million in revenue by 2028.

items.title: ALL IN Expands with Satellite Events in Vancouver and Toronto
items.summary: Canada's largest technology gathering, ALL IN, announced the launch of ALL IN Talks satellite events in Vancouver (April 15) and Toronto (May 28) to accelerate technology adoption and strengthen Canada's innovation ecosystem.
